info.description: This problem set focuses on usage and mechanisms of antibacterial agents. The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the "Info and Links".

q1.prompt: An 8-month-old infant is diagnosed with a rickettsial infection. Which of the following best describes the clinical reasoning for avoiding tetracyclines, such as doxycycline, in this patient population?

options.label: Structural displacement of bilirubin causing kernicterus
High risk of acute hemolytic anemia in patients with G6PD deficiency
Potential for permanent damage to developing cartilage and tendons
Risk of Gray Baby Syndrome due to lack of liver enzymes
Permanent discoloration of teeth and inhibition of bone growth

q2.prompt: Which of the following describes the primary mechanism of action for fluoroquinolones?

options.label: Premature release of peptide chains from the 30S ribosome
Irreversible binding to the 23S rRNA of the 50S ribosomal subunit
Inhibition of bacterial topoisomerase II (DNA gyrase) and IV
Structural analogy to para-aminobenzoic acid in folate synthesis
Inhibition of the beta-subunit of DNA-dependent RNA polymerase

q3.prompt: A newborn develops a purulent ocular discharge and erythematous conjunctiva 13 days after birth. Which antibiotic is the drug of choice for this condition, caused by *Chlamydia trachomatis*?

q4.prompt: Which molecular modification of the cell wall precursor is responsible for the resistance mechanism in Vancomycin-Resistant Enterococci (VRE)?

options.label: Production of a novel penicillin-binding protein (PBP2a)
Methylation of the 23S ribosomal RNA subunit
Alteration of the terminal D-Ala-D-Ala to D-Ala-D-Lac
Replacement of the terminal D-Ala with D-Glutamate

q5.prompt: A patient with known Glucose-6-Phosphate Dehydrogenase (G6PD) deficiency requires treatment for a simple urinary tract infection. Which of the following drugs must be avoided to prevent acute hemolytic anemia?

q6.prompt: A patient being treated for pulmonary tuberculosis develops reduced visual acuity and an inability to distinguish red from green. Which of the following drugs is responsible for this effect?

q7.prompt: A hospitalized patient receiving high-dose gentamicin develops new-onset tinnitus and an elevated serum creatinine. Which bacterial structure is the primary target of this drug?

q8.prompt: For which of the following clinical reasons is nitrofurantoin contraindicated in patients with a creatinine clearance (Cr_(Cl)) less than 60 mL/min?

options.label: Potential to cause irreversible bone growth inhibition
Failure to achieve therapeutic concentrations in the urine
Risk of serotonin syndrome due to MAO inhibition
Increased risk of developing Achilles tendon rupture

q9.prompt: Which broad-spectrum antibiotic inhibits peptidyl transferase at the 50S subunit but is associated with the risk of "Gray Baby Syndrome" and aplastic anemia?

q10.prompt: Which cell wall component synthesis is inhibited by isoniazid (INH) in the treatment of Mycobacterium tuberculosis?
